common.binary

Class ByteArrayReaderOld

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream
      • common.binary.ByteArrayReaderOld
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    BinaryInput, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class ByteArrayReaderOld
extends AbstractBinaryStream
implements BinaryInput
    Performs in-memory reading of primitive data types from a byte array.

    Supports reading of signed and unsigned integers, floating-point numbers, and byte sequences with configurable byte order (big-endian or little-endian).

    Since:
    8 June 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field Description
      private int baseIndex  
      private byte[] buffer  
      private long bufferIndex  

      • Fields inherited from class common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream

        byteOrder, positionStack

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array using big-endian byte order.
      ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf, java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array using the specified byte order.
      ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf, int offset)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using big-endian byte order.
      ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf, int offset, java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using the specified byte order.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void close()
      Closes the reader.
      private byte getByte(long position)
      Returns a single byte from the array at the specified relative position.
      private byte[] getBytes(long position, int length)
      Copies and returns a sub-array from the byte array, starting from the specified position.
      long getCurrentPosition()
      Returns the current read position.
      long length()
      Returns the readable length of this reader.
      byte peek(long offset)
      Retrieves the data at the specified offset within the byte array without advancing the current position.
      byte[] peek(long offset, int length)
      Retrieves a sub-array of bytes from the specified absolute offset without advancing the current position.
      byte readByte()
      Reads a single signed byte from the current position and advances the current position by 1.
      byte[] readBytes(int length)
      Reads a sequence of bytes from the current position and advances the current position by the requested length.
      double readDouble()
      Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 8.
      float readFloat()
      Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 4.
      int readInteger()
      Reads a 32-bit signed integer value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 4.
      long readLong()
      Reads a 64-bit signed long value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 8.
      short readShort()
      Reads a 16-bit signed short value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 2.
      java.lang.String readString()
      Reads a null-terminated string using the default ISO-8859-1 character encoding.
      java.lang.String readString(java.nio.charset.Charset charset)
      Reads a null-terminated string decoded via the specified text character set.
      int readUnsignedByte()
      Reads an 8-bit unsigned integer from the current position and advances the current position by 1.
      long readUnsignedInteger()
      Reads a 32-bit unsigned integer value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 4.
      int readUnsignedInteger24()
      Reads a 24-bit unsigned integer (3 bytes) from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 3.
      int readUnsignedShort()
      Reads a 16-bit unsigned short from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 2.
      private long readValue(int numBytes)
      Reads a value composed of the specified number of bytes from the current position using the configured byte order.
      void seek(long pos)
      Moves to the specified position within the byte array.
      private void validateByteIndex(long position, int length)
      Validates that the specified byte range is within the readable bounds.

      • Methods inherited from class common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream

        checkBounds, getByteOrder, hasRemaining, hasRemaining, mark, remaining, reset, setByteOrder, skip

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

      • Methods inherited from interface common.binary.BinaryInput

        getByteOrder, mark, reset, setByteOrder, skip

    • Field Detail

      • buffer

        private final byte[] buffer

      • baseIndex

        private final int baseIndex

      • bufferIndex

        private long bufferIndex

    • Constructor Detail

      • ByteArrayReaderOld

        public ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf,
                          int offset,
                          java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using the specified byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array
        offset - the starting offset within the byte array
        order - the byte order for interpreting the input bytes, using either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the byte array is null or empty
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if offset is negative or greater than the length of the byte array
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if order is null

      • ByteArrayReaderOld

        public ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array using big-endian byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array

      • ByteArrayReaderOld

        public ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf,
                          java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array using the specified byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array
        order - the byte order for interpreting the input bytes, using either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN

      • ByteArrayReaderOld

        public ByteArrayReaderOld(byte[] buf,
                          int offset)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using big-endian byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array
        offset - the starting offset within the byte array

    • Method Detail

      • close

        public void close()
           throws java.io.IOException
        Closes the reader.

        This implementation performs no action because the reader operates entirely on an in-memory byte array.

        Specified by:
        close in interface BinaryInput
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • length

        public long length()
        Returns the readable length of this reader.
        Specified by:
        length in interface BinaryInput
        Specified by:
        length in class AbstractBinaryStream
        Returns:
        the number of bytes from the configured base offset to the end of the byte array

      • getCurrentPosition

        public long getCurrentPosition()
        Returns the current read position.
        Specified by:
        getCurrentPosition in interface BinaryInput
        Specified by:
        getCurrentPosition in class AbstractBinaryStream
        Returns:
        the current position relative to the configured base offset

      • seek

        public void seek(long pos)
        Moves to the specified position within the byte array.
        Specified by:
        seek in interface BinaryInput
        Specified by:
        seek in class AbstractBinaryStream
        Parameters:
        pos - the position to move to
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the position is invalid

      • readByte

        public byte readByte()
              throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a single signed byte from the current position and advances the current position by 1.
        Specified by:
        readByte in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed 8-bit byte value (-128 to 127)
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if the reader index has reached the end of the readable buffer bounds
        java.io.IOException

      • readUnsignedByte

        public int readUnsignedByte()
                     throws java.io.IOException
        Reads an 8-bit unsigned integer from the current position and advances the current position by 1.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedByte in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the unsigned 8-bit value (0-255) represented as an integer
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if the reader index has reached the end of the readable buffer bounds
        java.io.IOException

      • readBytes

        public byte[] readBytes(int length)
                 throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a sequence of bytes from the current position and advances the current position by the requested length.
        Specified by:
        readBytes in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        length - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        a newly allocated byte array containing the copied data payload
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer bytes remain in the stream than the requested length specifies
        java.io.IOException

      • readShort

        public short readShort()
                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 16-bit signed short value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 2.
        Specified by:
        readShort in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed short value (-32768 to 32767)
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 2 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readUnsignedShort

        public int readUnsignedShort()
                      throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 16-bit unsigned short from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 2.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedShort in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the unsigned short value (0 to 65535) represented as an integer
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 2 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readInteger

        public int readInteger()
                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 32-bit signed integer value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 4.
        Specified by:
        readInteger in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed 32-bit integer value
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 4 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readUnsignedInteger

        public long readUnsignedInteger()
                         throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 32-bit unsigned integer value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 4.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedInteger in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the unsigned 32-bit integer value represented as a long (0 to 4,294,967,295)
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 4 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readUnsignedInteger24

        public int readUnsignedInteger24()
                          throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 24-bit unsigned integer (3 bytes) from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 3.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedInteger24 in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the 24-bit unsigned value represented as an integer (0 to 16,777,215)
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 3 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readLong

        public long readLong()
              throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 64-bit signed long value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 8.
        Specified by:
        readLong in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed 64-bit long value
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 8 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readFloat

        public float readFloat()
                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 4.
        Specified by:
        readFloat in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the single-precision float value
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 4 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • readDouble

        public double readDouble()
                  throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point value from the current position using the configured byte order and advances the current position by 8.
        Specified by:
        readDouble in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the double value
        Throws:
        java.io.EOFException - if fewer than 8 bytes remain available in the stream buffer
        java.io.IOException

      • peek

        public byte peek(long offset)
        Retrieves the data at the specified offset within the byte array without advancing the current position.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        offset - the offset relative to the start of the readable data
        Returns:
        the byte of data fetched from the offset
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the targeted offset location falls outside of the buffer bounds

      • peek

        public byte[] peek(long offset,
                   int length)
        Retrieves a sub-array of bytes from the specified absolute offset without advancing the current position.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        offset - the offset relative to the start of the readable data
        length - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        a new byte array containing the requested data block segment
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the targeted offset slice window falls outside of the buffer bounds

      • readString

        public java.lang.String readString()
        Reads a null-terminated string using the default ISO-8859-1 character encoding.
        Specified by:
        readString in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the decoded string excluding the terminating null character
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the end of the buffer space is reached before discovering a null terminator

      • readString

        public java.lang.String readString(java.nio.charset.Charset charset)
        Reads a null-terminated string decoded via the specified text character set.

        Scans for 0x00 starting from the current position and advances the current position past the null terminator.

        Specified by:
        readString in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        charset - the text character encoding configuration to use for string decoding
        Returns:
        the decoded string excluding the null terminator
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the end of the buffer is reached before a null terminator
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if the given charset parameter is null

      • getByte

        private byte getByte(long position)
        Returns a single byte from the array at the specified relative position.
        Parameters:
        position - the index (relative to base offset) in the byte array
        Returns:
        the byte fetched from the position

      • getBytes

        private byte[] getBytes(long position,
                        int length)
        Copies and returns a sub-array from the byte array, starting from the specified position.
        Parameters:
        position - the index (relative to base offset) in the byte array
        length - the number of bytes to copy
        Returns:
        a new byte array containing the subset of the original array

      • readValue

        private long readValue(int numBytes)
                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a value composed of the specified number of bytes from the current position using the configured byte order.
        Parameters:
        numBytes - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        the computed value as a long
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the requested number of bytes is beyond the file's bounds

      • validateByteIndex

        private void validateByteIndex(long position,
                               int length)
        Validates that the specified byte range is within the readable bounds.
        Parameters:
        position - the position relative to the configured base offset
        length - the total number of bytes to check
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the position or target sequence range falls outside of bounds
